summary: Mealie already appears to have user-recipe favorite concepts in both backend and frontend: explorers reported UserToRecipe.is_favorite, existing /users/{id}/favorites/{slug} endpoints, a RecipeFavoriteBadge.vue, and a user favorites page. The requested input describes a new user_favorite_recipe table and new authenticated self endpoints, so the spec must decide whether to refactor existing behavior, add compatibility wrappers, or intentionally create a replacement system.

conflicts.description: Greenfield request conflicts with existing backend/frontend favorites code: requested new table and self endpoints vs reported UserToRecipe.is_favorite, /users/{id}/favorites/{slug}, favorite badge, and favorites page.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Treat as refactor/extension unless inspection proves otherwise; choose reuse, compatibility wrapper, or explicit replacement with migration/backfill.

conflicts.description: Persistence model conflict: existing UserToRecipe.is_favorite boolean association vs requested dedicated user_favorite_recipe join table.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Decide canonical storage; either keep existing field, migrate/backfill to new table, or temporarily support both with consistency tests.

conflicts.description: API path conflict: existing/UI paths appear user-id based (/users/{id}/favorites/{slug}) while input requires current-user self paths (/api/users/self/favorites/{recipe_slug}).
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Define canonical path and compatibility/deprecation behavior; update frontend client or preserve aliases.

conflicts.description: History perspective labels the feature greenfield because commit-grep found no favorite implementation, but static/UI findings indicate existing code.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Trust current code inspection for existence; use history only for migration/test conventions.

conflicts.description: Confirmed scope excludes frontend, but existing frontend likely calls favorite APIs and may break if backend paths/schema change without compatibility.
conflicts.resolution_suggestion: Keep backend-only scope with compatibility tests, or add minimal client/type regeneration or a follow-up UI task.

consolidated_artifacts.reason: Existing backend association/model reportedly contains is_favorite; inspect before adding or replacing user_favorite_recipe.
Existing user favorite endpoints reportedly include /users/{id}/favorites/{slug}; conflicts with requested self endpoint contract.
Existing frontend user API client already has favorite endpoints; requires compatibility or migration decision.
Existing heart/favorite toggle UI means favorites are not purely greenfield.
Existing user favorites page likely depends on old endpoint semantics.
Recipe responses must add favorited and favorite_count while preserving anonymous behavior.
Recipe list/detail routes must expose favorite fields and avoid N+1 query behavior.
Likely service touch point for current-user favorite state and aggregate favorite counts.
Required coverage for idempotency, anonymous behavior, cross-group 404, count, cascade cleanup, and pagination.
Migration location and naming convention if new table/backfill is chosen.
Input suggests this as repository layer for user favorite operations.
Possible new repository if dedicated favorite table is chosen.
Requested Pydantic schema location for self favorites API.
Generated API types; do not edit manually if frontend contract changes.
Use Mealie i18n for errors instead of hard-coded English.

consolidated_conventions: New table-backed features typically ship migration, DB model, repository, route/controller, schema, and integration/unit tests together in one PR.
Household/group isolation should be enforced in service/query logic and proven in integration or multitenant tests.
User-facing errors should use Mealie i18n message files instead of hard-coded English.
Pydantic/OpenAPI schemas and response_model definitions should be complete for FastAPI OpenAPI generation.
Recipe list enrichment must avoid N+1 by using joins, IN, aggregate subqueries, or EXISTS.
Frontend API types are generated via task dev:generate; do not manually edit frontend/app/lib/api/types/.
Frontend API clients live under frontend/app/lib/api/ and extend BaseAPI / BaseCRUDAPI.
